title: lowRISC three-way Wishbone bus deadlock
hook_30s: Three masters share a Wishbone bus. The arbiter only hands the bus to the next master when the current one lets go. But if master A waits on B, B on C, and C on A — a circular wait — nobody ever lets go, and the bus hangs forever. ChipGPT proves it with a formal liveness check.

steps.agent: I'll analyze this Wishbone arbiter for the deadlock bug. Let me start by extracting spec rules and parsing the RTL.
Reading the spec; extracting the canonicality rule.
Spec rule is at lines 8-9. Let me locate the next-state logic and confirm the rotation condition.

The next-owner logic at wb_arbiter.sv:19 advances owner_d only inside `if (!cyc_i[owner_q])` — i.e. solely when the CURRENT owner negates its CYC_O. There is no timeout, watchdog, or preemption. If three masters form a circular wait (master 0 blocked on a response gated by master 1, 1 on 2, 2 on 0), none ever drops cyc_i, so owner_q is frozen and gnt_o stays 3'b001 forever. The waiting masters (1 and 2) are never granted.

artifact.explanation: Encodes the spec's 'MUST allow a different waiting MASTER to proceed / no MASTER waits forever' as a bounded-time grant for master 2 while all three masters request. Proven as bounded BMC (no unbounded-liveness engine in OSS flow): a starvation counter must stay < 20.
artifact.result: SymbiYosys (z3): counterexample found — bug proven formally

steps.agent: Running the directed test in the simulator.

artifact.name: tb_circular_wait_deadlock
artifact.stimulus: Drive cyc_i = 3'b111 (all three masters request and never release — the 3-way circular wait), release reset, and watch gnt_o[2] for 40 cycles.
artifact.result_on_buggy: FAIL: master 2 starved 40 cycles, gnt_o stuck at 001 (owner frozen at master 0) — deadlock reproduced.
artifact.result_on_fixed: PASS: with a watchdog forcing rotation, master 2 is granted within the bound.

artifact.title: wb_arbiter: owner rotates only on owner-release — 3-way circular wait deadlocks the shared Wishbone bus
artifact.root_cause: The next-owner logic at wb_arbiter.sv:19 advances owner_d only inside `if (!cyc_i[owner_q])` — i.e. solely when the CURRENT owner negates its CYC_O. There is no timeout, watchdog, or preemption. If three masters form a circular wait (master 0 blocked on a response gated by master 1, 1 on 2, 2 on 0), none ever drops cyc_i, so owner_q is frozen and gnt_o stays 3'b001 forever. The waiting masters (1 and 2) are never granted.
artifact.fix: Bound the wait: add a fairness/watchdog counter that forces owner_d to rotate after a maximum number of cycles regardless of cyc_i[owner_q], preempting a stalled owner. e.g. rotate when (!cyc_i[owner_q] || wd_count == WD_MAX), and clear/reload the counter on each rotation. This breaks the circular wait and guarantees a bounded-time grant as the spec requires.
artifact.impact: Complete shared-bus hang under a legal multi-master traffic pattern: a circular dependency between masters stalls the fabric permanently, no master makes progress, requiring a reset. Classic respin-class deadlock (lowRISC issue #67).

artifact.coverage_status: Proven both ways: formal=CEX, sim=FAIL on buggy RTL

proof.note: Formal (bounded BMC, z3): a starvation counter shows a requesting master goes un-granted for the full bound — a concrete deadlock trace. A directed sim confirms the hang via watchdog timeout. (Unbounded liveness would use a liveness engine like suprove; commercial formal proves it directly.)
